segments.text: Lesson 40.

translations.zh-Hans: 第40课。

segments.text: Who's Who.

translations.zh-Hans: 真假难辨。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: How did the policeman discover that the whole thing was a joke?

translations.zh-Hans: 警察是怎样发现整件事是一场恶作剧的？

segments.text: It has never been explained why university students seem to enjoy practical jokes more than anyone else.

translations.zh-Hans: 为什么大学生似乎比任何人都更喜欢恶作剧，这一点从未得到解释。

segments.text: Students specialize in a particular type of practical joke, the hoax, inviting the fire brigade to put out a non-existent fire is a crude form of deception which no self-respecting student would ever indulge

translations.zh-Hans: 学生们尤其擅长一种叫作骗局的恶作剧；叫消防队去扑灭一场并不存在的火，是一种粗劣的欺骗，任何自重的学生都不屑于

segments.text: in.

translations.zh-Hans: 这么做。

segments.text: Students often create amusing situations which are funny to everyone except the victims.

translations.zh-Hans: 学生们常制造一些除了受害者外人人都觉得好笑的场面。

segments.text: When a student recently saw two workmen using a pneumatic drill outside his university, he immediately telephoned the police and informed them that two students dressed up as workmen were tearing up the road with a

translations.zh-Hans: 最近，一名学生看到两名工人在大学外使用风钻，立即打电话报警，说有两名学生装扮成工人，正用

segments.text: pneumatic drill.

translations.zh-Hans: 风钻挖坏路面。

segments.text: As soon as he had hung up, he went over to the workmen and told them that if a policeman ordered them to go away, they were not to take him seriously.

translations.zh-Hans: 挂断电话后，他马上走到工人身边，告诉他们如果有警察命令他们离开，千万不要当真。

segments.text: He added that a student had dressed up as a policeman and was playing all sorts of jokes on people.

translations.zh-Hans: 他又说，有个学生装扮成警察，正在到处捉弄人。

segments.text: Both the police and the workmen were grateful to the student for this piece of advance information.

translations.zh-Hans: 警察和工人都很感激这名学生事先通报消息。

segments.text: The student hid in an archway nearby where he could watch and hear everything that went on.

translations.zh-Hans: 学生躲进附近的一道拱门里，从那里可以看见并听清发生的一切。

segments.text: Sure enough, a policeman arrived on the scene and politely asked the workmen to go away.

translations.zh-Hans: 果然，一名警察赶到现场，礼貌地请工人离开。

segments.text: When he received a very rude reply from one of the workmen, he threatened to remove them by force.

translations.zh-Hans: 其中一名工人十分粗鲁地回绝后，警察威胁要强行赶走他们。

segments.text: The workmen told him to do as he pleased and the policeman telephoned for help.

translations.zh-Hans: 工人叫他悉听尊便，警察便打电话请求增援。

segments.text: Shortly afterwards, four more policemen arrived and remonstrated with the workmen.

translations.zh-Hans: 不久又来了四名警察，劝诫工人停止施工。

segments.text: As the men refused to stop working, the police attempted to seize the pneumatic drill.

translations.zh-Hans: 工人拒绝停工，警察便试图夺下风钻。

segments.text: The workmen struggled fiercely and one of them lost his temper.

translations.zh-Hans: 工人们奋力反抗，其中一人发了火。

segments.text: He threatened to call the police.

translations.zh-Hans: 他威胁说要报警。

segments.text: At this, the police pointed out ironically that this would hardly be necessary as the men were already under arrest.

translations.zh-Hans: 警察讽刺地指出，既然他们已经被捕，恐怕没必要再报警了。

segments.text: Pretending to speak seriously, one of the workmen asked if he might make a telephone call before being taken to the station.

translations.zh-Hans: 一名工人装出严肃的样子，问被带到警察局之前能否打个电话。

segments.text: Permission was granted and a policeman accompanied him to a payphone.

translations.zh-Hans: 警察同意了，一名警察陪他走到公用电话旁。

segments.text: Only when he saw that the man was actually telephoning the police did he realize that they had all been the victims of a hoax.

translations.zh-Hans: 直到看到那人真的在给警察打电话，他才意识到他们全都成了一场骗局的受害者。
